name: Neo-Recon
summary:
Neo-Recon
Neo-Recon is a fully automated, terminal-based pentesting and CTF assistant built in Bash. It offers live recon, vuln scanning, exploitation, password cracking, and reverse shell generation—all in one modular tool. Made for ethical hackers, students, and infosec enthusiasts needing speed, automation, and results.

documentation.readme: Neo-Recon Neo-Recon is a fully automated, terminal-based pentesting and CTF assistant built in Bash. It offers live recon, vuln scanning, exploitation, password cracking, and reverse shell generation—all in one modular tool. Made for ethical hackers, students, and infosec enthusiasts needing speed, automation, and results.
usage_sections: Automate reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, and password cracking
Act as a step-by-step companion for CTF challenges

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_osint_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the OSINT category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_osint_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the OSINT system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for Neo-Recon (Abr_ahamis/Neo-Recon). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for Neo-Recon. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with Axelaxl/exploiting-phone (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with 0xw01f/AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with wso2/phuzz (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
